title: Ranik Solusar
content:

Solusar's death must therefore must have occurred within that [[Time/Legends|time]] period.</ref>

{{Quote|Kam's father, Ranik Solusar, was a great Jedi Master.|Luke Skywalker|<ref name="ij">''[[I, Jedi]]''</ref>}}
'''Ranik Solusar''' was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] who served the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] and the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] up to and during the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]]. He [[Marriage/Legends|married]], and fathered a son, [[Kam Solusar|Kam]], against the wishes of the [[Jedi Council/Legends|Jedi Council]], and was disciplined for his actions. In [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]], he served on a [[Jedi assault team/Legends|strike team]] led by Master [[Mace Windu/Legends|Mace Windu]] during the opening confrontation of the Clone Wars, the [[First Battle of Geonosis/Legends|Battle of Geonosis]]. There, Solusar flew alongside several other Jedi in their [[Delta-7 Aethersprite-class light interceptor/Legends|starfighters]], and helped prevent the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederate]] ally [[Cavik Toth]] from deploying the deadly [[Bioweapon/Legends|bio-agent]] [[trihexalon]] on the surface of [[Geonosis/Legends|Geonosis]].

Solusar was later faced with [[Order 66/Legends|Order 66]]—an edict that ordered the [[Clone trooper/Legends|clone troopers]] serving the Jedi during the Clone Wars to murder the Jedi. Solusar evaded calamity, and was wanted by the emergent [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] in the days following the [[Declaration of a New Order]]. Solusar's name was on a list compiled by [[Imperial Intelligence/Legends|Imperial Intelligence]], and he was to be reported to the authorities if he was spotted by a civilian. Solusar was later hunted down by [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine's]] enforcer, the [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]] [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]]. The Dark Lord struck down Solusar, and Kam was later forced to serve the Empire. In time, however, Solusar's son was eventually redeemed by Luke Skywalker and eventually became a founding member of a [[new Jedi Order]].

==Biography==
===Master and son===
Ranik Solusar was a [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] of the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] who served the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]].<ref name="deII1" /><ref name="js" /> Against the will of the [[Jedi Council/Legends|Jedi Council]], he [[Marriage/Legends|married]], and sired a boy named [[Kam Solusar|Kam]]. As a result, he was reprimanded by the Council. Still, Solusar went on to [[Jedi training/Legends|train]] his [[Youngling/Legends|child]] in the ways of [[The Force/Legends|the Force]], although his instruction was informal.<ref name="negtc" /> Solusar's teaching methods stemmed from a different school of thought to those of [[Grand Master/Legends|Grand Master]] [[Yoda/Legends|Yoda]], focusing on resourcefulness and concentration, and he went on to train his son for several years.<ref name="jasb" />

===Clone Wars===
In [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]],<ref name="NEC">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' places the events of the film [[Star Wars: Episode II Attack of the Clones|''Star Wars:'' Episode II ''Attack of the Clones'']], which features the [[First Battle of Geonosis/Legends|First Battle of Geonosis]], in [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]].</ref> as the [[Separatist Crisis/Legends|Separatist Crisis]] continued to brew, [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]] was captured on the [[Planet/Legends|world]] of [[Geonosis/Legends|Geonosis]], which was a part of the seceding [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]]. As Kenobi was thought to be in danger, [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] Master [[Mace Windu/Legends|Mace Windu]] assembled a [[Jedi assault team/Legends|strike team]],<ref name="AotC">{{Film|II}}</ref> of which Solusar was a participant. Solusar, [[Pilot/Legends|piloting]] a [[Delta-7 Aethersprite-class light interceptor/Legends|Delta-7 ''Aethersprite''-class light interceptor]], flew alongside the likes of [[Darth Krayt|A'Sharad Hett]], [[Bant Eerin/Legends|Bant Eerin]], and [[The Dark Woman]] as part of Master [[Adi Gallia/Legends|Adi Gallia]]'s [[Zeta Squadron (Jedi Order)|Zeta Squadron]] to Geonosis, where the Jedi were [[First Battle of Geonosis/Legends|engaged]] by Confederate ally [[Cavik Toth]]'s [[Sabaoth Squadron/Legends|Sabaoth Squadron]].<ref name="js" />

While Windu and the bulk of the strike team liberated Kenobi and led the newly-formed [[Grand Army of the Republic/Legends|Grand Army of the Republic]] into combat against the Confederate [[Separatist Droid Army/Legends|droid army]] on the planet's surface,<ref name="AotC" /> Solusar and his compatriots continued to battle Toth's forces. Toth attempted to deploy the deadly [[Bioweapon/Legends|bioweapon]] [[trihexalon]] on the Geonosian surface, but Solusar and the other Jedi worked with Gallia to destroy the [[Hex Deployer]] and [[Sabaoth destroyer]] spacecraft. The [[Lok Revenants/Legends|Lok Revenants]], headed by the pirate [[Nym/Legends|Nym]], arrived to reinforce Solusar and the other Jedi, while Gallia was able to personally [[Death/Legends|kill]] Toth, who had joined the battle himself in his [[Cavik Toth's starfighter|personal starfighter]].<ref name="js" /> The battle was won by the Jedi and the Republic, although it heralded the opening of the pan-[[The galaxy/Legends|galactic]] [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]].<ref name="AotC" />

===The Purge===
[[File:KamSolusar-NEGTC.png|thumb|left|180px|Solusar's son and informal apprentice, Kam.]]
Just before the conclusion of the Clone Wars,<ref name="rots">{{Film|III}}</ref> in [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]]<ref name="NEC2">The events of the film [[Star Wars: Episode III Revenge of the Sith|''Star Wars'': Episode III ''Revenge of the Sith'']], which depicts the end of the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], are set in [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]], according to ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''.</ref> [[Supreme Chancellor/Legends|Supreme Chancellor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] issued [[Order 66/Legends|Order 66]]—in which the [[Clone trooper/Legends|clone troopers]] of the Grand Army turned against the Jedi who had been leading them into combat during the war.<ref name="rots" /> Solusar was able to evade the clone troopers, escaping with his life.<ref name="cswe">{{CSWECite|III|179|Solusar, Ranik}}</ref> Shortly afterward, Palpatine installed himself as [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Galactic Emperor]], and he [[Declaration of a New Order|reformed]] the Republic into the first [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]]. Simultaneously, he declared that the Jedi Knights were now enemies of the Empire, having [[Showdown on Coruscant|attempted]] to claim his life and take over the [[Galactic Senate/Legends|Galactic Senate]].<ref name="rots" /> Forced to become a fugitive, Solusar was wanted by [[Imperial Intelligence/Legends|Imperial Intelligence]]. On the [[HoloNet/Legends|HoloNet]], citizens were advised to turn Solusar in should they encounter him.<ref name="hnn">{{InsiderCite|84|Republic HoloNet News Special Inaugural Edition 16:5:241}}</ref> Solusar was driven apart from Kam by the altered state of affairs, bringing an end to his son's training.<ref name="jasb" />

Solusar was placed on an extensive list of missing Jedi, alongside [[Halagad Ventor]] and [[Kai Justiss]].<ref name="hnn" /> At some point before [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]],<ref name="death date" /> the Jedi Master was eventually slain by Palpatine's enforcer, the [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]], [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]].<ref name="ee1">[[Empire's End 1|''Empire's End'' 1]]</ref> Kam was later swayed into the service of the Empire, although he eventually defected to the cause of the emergent Jedi Knight [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]],<ref name="deII1" /> who had brought an end to both Palpatine and Vader.<ref name="rotj">{{Film|VI}}</ref> Kam went on to study under Skywalker, and was one of the founding members of Skywalker's [[New Jedi Order]].<ref name="ij" /> Kam passed on his father's teachings, which were utilized at Skywalker's [[Jedi Praxeum]].<ref name="jasb" />

==Personality and traits==
When Solusar, a Jedi Master, chose to marry and have a child while serving the Jedi Order, he clashed with the Jedi Council. As a result, he earned many reprimands for his actions.<ref name="negtc" /> While operating as an informal master to his son, Solusar worked from a different school of thought than other Jedi, such as Yoda, instead opting to focus on exercises that enhanced concentration skills and resourcefulness.<ref name="jasb" />

==Powers and abilities==
{{Quote|I was apprenticed to the great Jedi Master, ''Ranik Solusar''—my father.|Kam Solusar|<ref name="deII1" />}}
Solusar was heralded by his son as a "great" Jedi Master.<ref name="deII1" /> He was able to fly a Delta-7 interceptor, and did so successfully during the Battle of Geonosis.<ref name="js" /> His skills allowed him to escape Order 66<ref name="cswe" /> and evade death in the early days of the [[Great Jedi Purge/Legends|Great Jedi Purge]],<ref name="hnn" /> but did not allow him to overcome the [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] Darth Vader, and he was, as a result, killed.<ref name="ee1" />

==Behind the scenes==
Ranik Solusar was first mentioned in the [[Star Wars comic books|comic book]] [[Dark Empire II 1|''Dark Empire II'' 1]], written by [[Tom Veitch]] and published in [[1994]].<ref name="deII1" /> The character was later mentioned in the [[Empire's End 1|first issue]] of the [[1995]] ''[[Star Wars: Empire's End]]'' mini-series,<ref name="ee1" /> and the [[Michael A. Stackpole]] novel, ''[[I, Jedi]]''.<ref name="ij" /> Solusar was also briefly referenced in ''[[The Jedi Academy Sourcebook]]'', in regards to his instructional technique.<ref name="jasb" /> Eight years after his initial mention, he made his first appearance in the [[2002]] video game, ''[[Star Wars: Jedi Starfighter]]''. The character appears alongside several other Jedi from the wider [[Star Wars Legends|Expanded Universe]], including comics and novels, and is identified as the pilot of one of the Jedi starfighters at the Battle of Geonosis.<ref name="js" />

Later that year, the character was referenced in ''[[The New Essential Guide to Characters]]'', wherein Solusar's marriage and siring of a child, which conflicts with the more monastic portrayal of the Jedi in films such as [[Star Wars: Episode I The Phantom Menace|''Star Wars'': Episode I ''The Phantom Menace'']] and [[Star Wars: Episode II Attack of the Clones|''Star Wars'': Episode II ''Attack of the Clones'']], was addressed.<ref name="negtc" /> In [[2005]], the character was referenced once again, in the ''[[Republic HoloNet News Special Inaugural Edition 16:5:241]]'' article, published within [[Star Wars Insider 84|''Star Wars Insider'' 84]], which both tied into the back-story of the latest ''[[Star Wars]]'' film, [[Star Wars: Episode III Revenge of the Sith|''Star Wars'': Episode III ''Revenge of the Sith'']], and Solusar's earlier history established in ''Dark Empire II'' and ''Empire's End''.<ref name="hnn" />
